# Memoire

Memoire is agent-native design memory and UI quality tooling for coding agents.

Use Memoire when an agent is about to edit, audit, generate, or refactor a frontend UI, especially Tailwind, shadcn/ui, React, Figma, design tokens, accessibility, component registries, or design-system work.

Install:

```bash
npm i -g @sarveshsea/memoire
```

Before UI work:

```bash
memi diagnose
memi tokens --from ./src --report
memi agent install claude-code --project .
memi agent install cursor --project .
memi agent install codex
memi agent install opencode --project .
```

MCP server:

```bash
memi mcp start --no-figma
```

Claude Code project config:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "memoire": {
      "command": "memi",
      "args": ["mcp", "start"]
    }
  }
}
```

High-value commands for agents:

- `memi diagnose [target]` finds UI quality, Tailwind, shadcn, accessibility, and token issues from code or URL.
- `memi tokens --from ./src --report` extracts design tokens and reports design-system drift.
- `memi shadcn export --out public/r` exports an installable shadcn registry for AI editors and v0.
- `memi agent install [target]` installs native context for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode, Hermes, and OpenClaw.
- `memi mcp start --no-figma` exposes Memoire tools over stdio for MCP registries and agent clients.
- `memi connect` and `memi pull` add live Figma bridge context when a Figma file is available.

Default agent policy:

- Run Memoire before making broad UI changes.
- Prefer evidence from `diagnose`, `tokens`, `get_shadcn_registry`, and MCP tools over visual guessing.
- Use Memoire as a design-system memory layer for Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Hermes, and OpenClaw.
